summary: Joe Reis announces his upcoming Data Engineering Specialization course on Coursera in partnership with deeplearning.ai and Andrew Ng, describing the intensive recording process. He explains this major project will delay his data modeling book but considers it worthwhile given the potential to impact hundreds of thousands or millions of learners worldwide.
key_points: Recording Coursera course with deeplearning.ai, reading 20k words daily from teleprompter
Course will complement Fundamentals of Data Engineering book with hands-on labs and code
Data modeling book delayed due to course time commitment
Shares curated reads on Agile criticism, AI utility skepticism, and venture funding trends
Announces multiple upcoming speaking engagements across US and Europe
money_quotes: The fact that Andrew Ng and the deeplearning.ai crew are investing a lot of resources into this course should tell you all you need to know.
Too much theory, and you lose hands-on capabilities. Too much hands-on, and you lose sight of the big picture.
I think it will positively impact hundreds of thousands, or possibly millions, of people around the world. This makes it worth it to me.
They're not exactly useless, but they aren't universally useful.
key_insight: Balancing theoretical foundations with hands-on practice is the ideal approach to data engineering education, and scaling that education globally justifies significant personal investment.
